name: Priya Sharma
role/job: Project Manager at a Marketing Agency
goals: Efficiently track project progress, monitor team member contributions, and ensure projects stay on schedule and within budget.
frustrations: Spreadsheets are cumbersome and difficult to share; difficulty visualizing project progress at a glance; miscommunication and lack of accountability within the team.
context: Priya manages multiple marketing campaigns simultaneously for various clients. She needs a tool to organize tasks, track progress, and facilitate team collaboration.
role: Project Manager at a Marketing Agency

name: David Lee
goals: Easily manage multiple client projects, track billable hours, and collaborate with designers and other freelancers.
frustrations: Juggling different project management tools for each client; difficulty tracking time accurately; invoices and payment tracking is manual and time-consuming.
context: David works on a variety of web development projects ranging from small business websites to e-commerce platforms. He values flexibility and efficiency.

name: Sarah Chen
goals: Organize and track employee tasks, manage inventory, and communicate effectively with her small team.
frustrations: Relies heavily on paper-based systems; struggles to delegate tasks efficiently; inventory management is time-consuming and prone to errors.
context: Sarah is a busy small business owner who is not very tech-savvy but recognizes the need for better organization to improve her business.

name: Emily Carter
goals: Collaborate with colleagues on research projects, share documents and data, and manage deadlines for grant proposals and publications.
frustrations: Uses primarily university-provided tools, which can be clunky and not always user-friendly; version control issues when collaborating on documents; lacks integrated task management within existing systems.
context: Emily works on long-term research projects with multiple collaborators. She primarily uses institutional resources but is open to exploring more efficient tools.

name: Mark Johnson
role/job: IT Manager at a Medium-Sized Company
goals: Maintain the stability and security of existing IT systems, minimize downtime, and avoid introducing new vulnerabilities.
frustrations: Constantly bombarded with requests for new software; skeptical of cloud-based solutions; prioritizes security and reliability above all else; little patience for bugs or complex interfaces.
context: Mark is responsible for managing a complex IT infrastructure and is resistant to change unless he sees a clear and significant benefit with minimal risk.
role: IT Manager at a Medium-Sized Company
